name: creating-claude-hooks
description: Use when creating or publishing Claude Code hooks - covers executable format, event types, JSON I/O, exit codes, security requirements, and PRPM package structure
capabilities: Explain hook file format and executable requirements
Describe available event types (session-start, tool-call, user-prompt-submit)
Show JSON input and output conventions with exit codes
Demonstrate security best practices including input validation
Guide PRPM package structure for hook distribution
Provide example hooks in bash and TypeScript
author: AgentWorkforce
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown reference material for Claude Code hook creation. The skill provides educational examples showing hook format, event handling, input validation, and security best practices. No executable code, no file access, no network operations, and no external command execution at runtime. All static findings are false positives triggered by example code snippets within documentation.
valueStatement: Claude Code hooks enable automatic actions during AI sessions. This skill provides the reference guide for creating, securing, and publishing hooks that validate, log, and automate workflows.
userTitle: Create Claude Code hooks for automation
limitations: Does not execute or install hooks - provides reference only
Does not modify project files or configuration
Does not connect to external APIs or services
Does not provide pre-built hook binaries

bestPractices: Validate all JSON input from stdin before processing to prevent injection attacks
Keep PreToolUse hooks under 100ms to avoid blocking Claude Code execution
Use stderr for logging and debugging output to keep transcript clean
antiPatterns: Skipping input validation on JSON fields allows malformed data to cause errors
Running long operations synchronously blocks the AI session
Logging sensitive data to stdout exposes it in the transcript

faq.question: What languages can I use for Claude Code hooks?
faq.answer: Any executable language including bash, Python, Node.js, and TypeScript. The file must have a valid shebang.

faq.question: What are the available hook events?
faq.answer: Four events exist: session-start fires on new sessions, user-prompt-submit before prompts, tool-call before tool execution, and assistant-response after responses.

faq.question: How do I integrate hooks with existing tools?
faq.answer: Hooks receive event data via stdin as JSON. Parse this data and call external tools as needed. Use jq for bash or JSON.parse for JavaScript.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using third-party hooks?
faq.answer: Claude Code shows hook source code and requires confirmation before installing. Hooks run in your session context with your permissions.

faq.question: Why is my hook not executing?
faq.answer: Common causes: missing shebang line, file not executable (chmod +x), syntax errors, or taking too long to respond. Check stderr output.

faq.question: How do hooks differ from skills?
faq.answer: Hooks are executable scripts that run automatically on events. Skills are context-loaded reference guides activated by conversation topics.
